description: Letter from Ṣedaqa Shāmī, in Jerusalem, to his brother-in-law Yosef Shammash, presumably in Fustat/Cairo. In Hebrew and Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: Late, probably no earlier than 14th century (based on paleography and format). Mentions the arrival of Qaraites. The writer uses jōza (for classical Arabic zawja) for wife. (S. D. Goitein, Mediterranean Society, 3:161, 462) EMS On verso another inscription, in different hand and ink, with a date (only the day of the month), also mentions Yosef the beadle (in Judaeo-Arabic)
description_he: מכתב מצדקה שאמי, ירושלים, לגיסו יוסף שמש, פוסטאט/קהיר. בעברית ויהודית-ערבית. כנראה לא לפני המאה הארבע עשרה. מוזכרת הגעת קראים.
